name: Kvas<mint-Flavored Bread Beer Soup>
recipeInstructions:
NOTE Water must be hot as it will kill the yeast.
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees F. Cube bread and spread on a cookie sheet. Place in oven for 1 hour.
2. Bring 6 quarts of water to a boil and drop in the cubed bread. Remove from heat, cover with a towel, and allow to sit at room temperature for 8 hours.
3. Strain through a fine sieve by pressing moisture from the bread.
4. In a separate bowl, sprinkle yeast and 1/4 teaspoon sugar over lukewarm water. Stir to dissolve completely. Set aside in a warm place covered with a towel for about 10-12 minutes or until mixture doubles in volume.
5. Add mint leaves and remaining sugar, stir well. Re-cover with towel and set aside for another 8-12 hours at room temperature.
6. Strain the mixture through a fine sieve again.
7. Pour into a 1-gallon container, add raisins, cover top with plastic wrap, secure with rubber band, and place in cool spot (not cold) for 4-5 days or until raisins are floating and sediment has sunk to bottom.
8. Pour off clear amber liquid and rebottle in clean jug or bottles. Refrigerate until ready to use. In Russia, this is a beverage as well as chilled soup stock.
